sentences.sentence: Staying up an hour or two past bedtime makes it far harder for kids to learn, say scientists who deprived youngsters of sleep and tested whether their teachers could tell the difference.
sentences.translation: 科学家表示，熬夜一两个小时会使孩子更难学习；这些科学家曾让孩子们睡眠不足，并测试他们的老师是否能察觉其中的差异。

structure.text: Staying up an hour or two past bedtime
makes it far harder for kids to learn
who deprived youngsters of sleep and tested whether their teachers could tell the difference

difficult_points: 句首动名词短语作主语。
who 从句修饰 scientists，内部 whether 从句作 tested 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: They could.
sentences.translation: 老师们确实能察觉。
difficult_points: They 指上一句中的 teachers。
could 后省略 tell the difference。

sentences.sentence: If parents want their children to thrive academically, "Getting them to sleep on time is as important as getting them to school on time," said psychologist Gahan Fallone, who conducted the research at Brown Medical School.
sentences.translation: 心理学家加汉·法隆在布朗医学院开展了这项研究，他表示：‘如果家长希望孩子在学业上取得成功，那么让他们按时睡觉与按时上学同样重要。’

structure.text: If parents want their children to thrive academically
Getting them to sleep on time is as important as getting them to school on time
who conducted the research at Brown Medical School

difficult_points: If 从句提出家长希望孩子学业发展的条件。
引语中的两个动名词短语通过 as...as 比较；who 修饰 Gahan Fallone。

sentences.sentence: The study, unveiled Thursday at an American Medical Association (AMA) science writers meeting, was conducted on healthy children who had no evidence of sleep- or learning-related disorders.
sentences.translation: 这项研究于周四在美国医学会（AMA）科学作家会议上公布，研究对象是健康的儿童，他们没有睡眠或学习相关障碍的迹象。

structure.text: unveiled Thursday at an American Medical Association (AMA) science writers meeting
who had no evidence of sleep- or learning-related disorders

difficult_points: unveiled 过去分词短语补充研究公布的时间和场合。
who 从句修饰 healthy children，说明受试者没有相关障碍。

sentences.sentence: Difficulty paying attention was among the problems the sleepy youngsters faced -raising the question of whether sleep deprivation could prove even worse for people with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, or ADHD.
sentences.translation: 注意力难以集中是这些睡眠不足的青少年所面临的问题之一——这引发了一个疑问：睡眠剥夺对患有注意力缺陷多动障碍（ADHD）的人而言，是否会带来更严重的影响。

structure.text: whether sleep deprivation could prove even worse for people with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, or ADHD

difficult_points: the sleepy youngsters faced 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 problems。
破折号后的 raising 表结果，whether 从句说明由此产生的问题；-raising 是源文连写异常。

sentences.sentence: Fallone now is studying that question, and suspects that sleep problems "could hit children with ADHD as a double whammy".
sentences.translation: 法隆目前正在研究这一问题，并怀疑睡眠问题“可能会对患有多动症的儿童造成双重打击”。

structure.text: now is studying that question, and suspects
that sleep problems "could hit children with ADHD as a double whammy"

difficult_points: that question 回指睡眠不足对 ADHD 人群影响是否更严重。
that 从句作 suspects 的宾语，double whammy 比喻双重打击。

expressions.text: hit somebody as a double whammy

sentences.sentence: Sleep experts have long warned that Americans of all ages do not get enough shuteye.
sentences.translation: 睡眠专家长期以来一直警告称，各个年龄段的美国人都没有获得足够的睡眠。

structure.text: that Americans of all ages do not get enough shuteye

difficult_points: 现在完成时表示这一警告长期持续至今。
that 从句作 warned 的宾语；shuteye 是口语中的睡眠。

sentences.sentence: Sleep is important for health, bringing a range of benefits that, as Shakespeare put it, "knits up the ravelled sleave of care".
sentences.translation: 睡眠对健康至关重要，它带来诸多益处，正如莎士比亚所言，“缝合了烦忧的裂痕”。

structure.text: that, as Shakespeare put it, "knits up the ravelled sleave of care"

difficult_points: bringing 短语补充说明睡眠带来的结果。
that 修饰 benefits；as Shakespeare put it 是插入语，sleave 的异常拼写保留自源文。

sentences.sentence: Not getting enough is linked to a host of problems, from car crashes as drivers doze off to crippled memory and inhibited creativity.
sentences.translation: 睡眠不足与一系列问题相关，从司机打瞌睡引发的车祸，到记忆力受损和创造力受抑。

structure.text: is linked to a host of problems
from car crashes as drivers doze off to crippled memory and inhibited creativity

difficult_points: Not getting enough 是动名词短语，省略的宾语是 sleep。
from...to...列出问题范围，as 引导司机打瞌睡时的伴随情境。

sentences.sentence: Exactly how much sleep correlates with school performance is hard to prove.
sentences.translation: 究竟多少睡眠与学业表现相关，这一点很难证实。

structure.text: Exactly how much sleep correlates with school performance

difficult_points: how much 引导的从句整体作主语。
correlates with 表示睡眠量与学业表现存在相关性，而非直接因果。

sentences.sentence: So, Brown researchers set out to test whether teachers could detect problems with attention and learning when children stayed up late - even if the teachers had no idea how much sleep their students actually got.
sentences.translation: 因此，布朗大学的研究人员着手测试：当孩子们熬夜时，教师是否能察觉其注意力和学习方面的问题——即使教师并不知道学生实际获得了多少睡眠。

structure.text: whether teachers could detect problems with attention and learning
even if the teachers had no idea how much sleep their students actually got

difficult_points: whether 从句作 test 的宾语，when 从句限定问题出现的时间。
even if 引导让步从句，其中 how much sleep 从句作 idea 的同位内容。

sentences.sentence: They recruited seventy-four 6- to 12-year-olds from Rhode Island and southern Massachusetts for the three-week study.
sentences.translation: 他们从罗德岛州和马萨诸塞州南部招募了74名6至12岁的儿童，参与这项为期三周的研究。

structure.text: from Rhode Island and southern Massachusetts

difficult_points: They 指布朗大学的研究人员。
6- to 12-year-olds 是带范围修饰的复合名词，指6至12岁儿童。

sentences.sentence: For one week, the youngsters went to bed and woke up at their usual times.
sentences.translation: 其中一周，这些孩子按照平时的作息时间上床睡觉和起床。

structure.text: went to bed and woke up

difficult_points: For one week 交代三周实验中的一个阶段。
went to bed 与 woke up 是并列谓语，共用主语。

sentences.sentence: They already were fairly good sleepers, getting nine to 9.5 hours of sleep a night.
sentences.translation: 他们原本就睡得相当不错，每晚能睡9到9.5小时。

structure.text: getting nine to 9.5 hours of sleep a night

difficult_points: good sleepers 指平时睡眠状况良好的人，而非睡眠能力。
getting 分词短语补充说明其每晚实际睡眠时长。

sentences.sentence: Another week, they were assigned to spend no fewer than ten hours in bed a night.
sentences.translation: 另一周，他们被要求每晚在床上至少待满10小时。

structure.text: were assigned to spend no fewer than ten hours in bed a night

difficult_points: Another week 是省略介词 in 的时间状语。
were assigned to 表示研究人员给受试者安排任务；no fewer than 强调不少于。

sentences.sentence: The other week, they were kept up later than usual: First -and second-graders were in bed no more than eight hours and the older children no more than 6.5 hours.
sentences.translation: 还有一周，他们比平常睡得更晚：一、二年级学生每晚睡眠不超过8小时，年龄较大的孩子则不超过6.5小时。

structure.text: were kept up later than usual
First -and second-graders were in bed no more than eight hours
the older children no more than 6.5 hours

difficult_points: 冒号后具体说明 kept up later 的睡眠限制。
后一个并列分句省略 were in bed；First -and 是源文排版异常。

sentences.sentence: In addition to parents' reports, the youngsters wore motion detecting wrist monitors to ensure compliance.
sentences.translation: 除了家长的报告外，孩子们还佩戴了可检测动作的手腕监测仪，以确保他们遵守规定。
difficult_points: In addition to 引出除监测设备以外的另一信息来源。
to ensure compliance 是目的不定式，说明佩戴监测器是为确认按要求作息。

sentences.sentence: Teachers were not told how much the children slept or which week they stayed up late, but rated the students on a variety of performance measures each week.
sentences.translation: 教师们并未被告知孩子们的睡眠时长或哪一周他们熬夜了，但每周都会根据一系列表现指标对学生进行评分。

structure.text: how much the children slept or which week they stayed up late
but rated the students on a variety of performance measures each week

difficult_points: how much 与 which week 引导两个并列的间接疑问从句。
but 连接被动的知情限制与教师主动评分，体现盲法设计。

sentences.sentence: The teachers reported significantly more academic problems during the week of sleep deprivation, the study, which will be published in the journal Sleep in December, concluded.
sentences.translation: 该研究总结道，教师们报告称，在睡眠剥夺的那一周，学生出现了明显更多的学业问题；这项研究将于十二月发表在《睡眠》期刊上。

structure.text: reported significantly more academic problems during the week of sleep deprivation
which will be published in the journal Sleep in December

difficult_points: 句子采用倒装式引语结构：真正主干是 the study concluded，前面的内容是结论。
which 修饰 the study，补充其计划发表的期刊与时间。

sentences.sentence: Students who got eight hours of sleep or less a night were more forgetful, had the most trouble learning new lessons, and had the most problems paying attention, reported Fallone, now at the Forest Institute of Professional Psychology.
sentences.translation: 福隆（现任职于森林专业心理学院）报告称，每晚睡眠时间少于八小时的学生更容易健忘，学习新课程最为困难，且注意力集中方面的问题也最多。

structure.text: Students who got eight hours of sleep or less a night
who got eight hours of sleep or less a night
were more forgetful, had the most trouble learning new lessons, and had the most problems paying attention

difficult_points: who 从句限定每晚睡眠不超过八小时的学生。
三个并列谓语描述健忘、学习新课困难和注意力问题；reported Fallone 是后置消息来源。

sentences.sentence: Sleep has long been a concern of educators.
sentences.translation: 睡眠问题长期以来一直是教育工作者关注的重点。

structure.text: has long been a concern of educators

difficult_points: 现在完成时强调教育界长期持续关注睡眠问题。
a concern of educators 表示教育工作者关注的事项，而非他们自身的忧虑。

sentences.sentence: Potter-Burns Elementary School sends notes to parents reminding them to make sure students get enough sleep prior to the school's yearly achievement testing.
sentences.translation: 波特-伯恩斯小学会向家长发送通知，提醒他们在学校年度学业评估测试前确保学生获得充足睡眠。

structure.text: reminding them to make sure students get enough sleep
prior to the school's yearly achievement testing

difficult_points: reminding 分词短语说明通知的作用，them 指 parents。
make sure 后接省略 that 的宾语从句；prior to 表示在年度测试之前。

sentences.sentence: Another school considers it important enough to include in the school's monthly newsletters.
sentences.translation: 另一所学校认为此事足够重要，因此将其纳入学校的月度通讯中。

structure.text: to include in the school's monthly newsletters

difficult_points: it 指睡眠问题，作 considers 的形式宾语。
important enough to... 表示重要到足以纳入月度简报；include 后省略宾语 it。

sentences.sentence: Definitely, there is an impact on students' performance if they come to school tired.
sentences.translation: 毫无疑问，如果学生带着疲惫来到学校，其学业表现会受到影响。

structure.text: there is an impact on students' performance
if they come to school tired

difficult_points: there is 是存在句，真正信息核心是 an impact on students' performance。
if 引导条件从句，tired 是主语补足语，说明到校时的状态。

sentences.sentence: However, the findings may change physician practice, said Dr. Regina Benjamin, a family physician in Bayou La Batre, who reviewed the data at the Thursday's AMA meeting.
sentences.translation: 然而，这些研究发现可能会改变医生的诊疗方式，周四在美国医学会（AMA）会议上审阅了相关数据的拜尤拉巴特雷镇家庭医生雷吉娜·本杰明博士表示。

structure.text: the findings may change physician practice
a family physician in Bayou La Batre
who reviewed the data at the Thursday's AMA meeting

difficult_points: However 后的陈述是前置引语内容，主句谓语 said 位于其后。
a family physician 是同位语；who 修饰 Benjamin，说明她审阅数据的场合。

sentences.sentence: "I don't ask about sleep" when evaluating academically struggling students, she noted.
sentences.translation: 她指出：“在评估学业困难的学生时，我过去并不询问他们的睡眠情况。”
difficult_points: 引语被前置，she noted 是后置报告分句。
when evaluating 省略主语 she 和 be 动词，完整含义是她评估学业困难学生时。

sentences.sentence: "I'm going to start."
sentences.translation: “我今后会开始这么做。”
difficult_points: be going to 表示说话者作出的未来打算。
start 后省略了 ask about sleep，需结合上一句补全。

sentences.sentence: So how much sleep do kids need?
sentences.translation: 那么孩子们到底需要多少睡眠呢？
difficult_points: how much sleep 被前置，用于询问所需睡眠量。
do 是一般现在时疑问句的助动词，实际谓语是 need。

sentences.sentence: Recommended amounts range from about ten to eleven hours a night for young elementary students to 8.5 hours for teens.
sentences.translation: 建议的睡眠时长范围从低年级小学生每晚约10至11小时，到青少年每晚8.5小时不等。

structure.text: range from about ten to eleven hours a night for young elementary students to 8.5 hours for teens

difficult_points: Recommended 是过去分词作定语，表示建议的睡眠量。
range from...to... 同时对比低年级小学生和青少年的建议时长。

sentences.sentence: Fallone insists that his own second-grader get ten hours a night, even when it meant dropping soccer - season that practice did not start until 7:30 — too late for her to fit in dinner and time to wind down before she needed to be snoozing.
sentences.translation: 福隆坚持让自己的二年级孩子每晚睡足10小时，即使这意味着要放弃足球训练——因为那个赛季的训练直到晚上7点半才开始，对她来说太晚了，无法兼顾晚餐和睡前放松的时间。

structure.text: that his own second-grader get ten hours a night
even when it meant dropping soccer
too late for her to fit in dinner and time to wind down

difficult_points: insists 后的 that 从句使用动词原形 get，属于建议性虚拟语气。
源文 soccer - season that practice 语法残缺；破折号后的 too late 说明训练时间导致无法安排晚餐和睡前放松。

sentences.sentence: "It's tough," he acknowledged, but "parents must believe in the importance of sleep."
sentences.translation: “这很难，”他承认道，“但家长们必须相信睡眠的重要性。”

structure.text: but "parents must believe in the importance of sleep."

difficult_points: 两个直接引语由 but 连接，形成困难与必要性的转折。
he acknowledged 插入在引语之间，说明前半句的说话者及态度。
